div.h1: Command Buffer Timeline: Safe Structural Changes

div.p: Temporal Separation of Concerns The command buffer creates a temporal boundary between reading and writing:

ul.li: During T0 (read phase): The entity-component graph's structure is frozen. Systems can mutate component data (Position.x += vel.dx), but cannot change which entities exist or which components they have.

During T2 (write phase): All structural changes are applied at once. No systems are running, so no iterators can be invalidated. This pattern eliminates an entire class of bugs: iterator invalidation, use-after-free, and "entity #5 became entity #5" confusion.

div.p: Atomic Application All changes applied in command order

ul.li:
Structure is IMMUTABLE:
No entity spawn
No entity destroy
No component add/remove

div.p: MUTATION PHASE Apply all queued commands:
